name: once
description: The standalone lodash.once package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want a small but exact once-only invocation contract without another dependency.
tagline: Run a function once and reuse the result.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.once file in both raw and normalized forms. The point is to vendor the exact one-time invocation behavior instead of rewriting it case by case.
nativeAlternative: A closure can implement a one-time call quickly, but teams often still prefer keeping a known utility contract instead of rewriting it repeatedly.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
